title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in March
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Bambola, a new Italian supper club in Seaport, features Roman cuisine by chef Bartolo Bruzzaniti and Italian-inspired cocktails.
James Beard Award winner Tony Messina helms Common Craft's new South Boston location with a focus on immersive culinary experiences.
Lanikai pop-up in Cambridge serves authentic Hawaiian poke with unique offerings like sushi hand rolls and Hawaiian specialties.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about Tuesday’s ‘wintry mix’
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A wintry mix of snow, sleet, and freezing rain is expected to impact travel in Boston on Tuesday.
Boston meteorologists predict light snow transitioning to sleet and rain by Tuesday evening as temperatures rise.
Cold weather will give way to milder conditions later in the week, with rain expected Thursday into Friday.

title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics to a 114-98 win over the 76ers with 27 points and 17 rebounds.
Jaylen Brown contributed 27 points, eight rebounds, and eight assists, showcasing improved passing skills.
The Celtics, strong on rebounding, grabbed 59 rebounds, helping secure the second-best rebounding percentage in the NBA.

title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate rose 3.5% in 2024 due to factors like mandatory and medical releases, according to Parole Board Chair Angelo Gomez.
Commonwealth v. Sheldon Mattis ruling in 2024 led to increased parole rates for 'youthful offenders' previously sentenced to life without parole.
In 2024, life sentence parole hearings had a 72% approval rate, up from 57% in 2023, based on Parole Board reports.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An uncirculated photo of Rosalie Miller was released to help solve her 1997 homicide case in New Hampshire.
Miller was last seen in December 1996 and found dead from strangulation in January 1997 in Auburn.
Officials urge anyone with information on Miller or the area of Londonderry Turnpike to contact the Cold Case Unit.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, accused of murdering Javon Lawson in Cranston, RI, amid alleged dispute over a mutual acquaintance.
Sousa allegedly fled the murder scene in a white Infiniti, later causing a fatal crash in Swansea, Massachusetts.
The crash resulted in the deaths of two people; Sousa was found injured with a pistol and ammunition in his vehicle.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are interested in trading for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, needing significant assets to secure him.
Robert Thomas boasts over 80 points in the last two seasons, making him a valuable addition to the Bruins' roster.
The Bruins have several first-round picks and prospects but a Thomas trade could deplete future assets significantly.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: The Maine Board of Pesticides Control approved a $3,000 settlement for tree poisoning without admitting guilt, involving a Brooklyn couple.
Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett allegedly killed neighbor Ruth Graham's trees for a better view, which they purchased in Rockport in 2017.
Ruth Graham's son suggested a donation to Maine coastal preservation and urged the couple to address the issue publicly.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Texas A&M WR KC Concepcion showcased strong route-running skills at the NFL Combine, enhancing his potential as a first-round pick.
Edge rushers Dani Dennis-Sutton and Malachi Lawrence achieved high relative athletic scores, positioning them as potential Day 2 draft choices.
Vanderbilt's Eli Stowers excelled with record-setting jumps, reinforcing his status as a top tight end prospect for the Patriots in draft rounds 1-2.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The New England Patriots are unlikely to trade major draft capital for a star player this offseason.
The Patriots are reportedly interested in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby, but trades may require hefty offers.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasized draft and development as key strategies, with Patriots holding 11 picks in the 2026 NFL Draft.
